description: Budget-aware Stage 2 pipeline: 8x1xH100 parallel sanity/screen, 1xH100 final proxy, optional 8xH100 champion confirmation.

slots.name: trunk_control_seq4096
slots.why: Clean public training trunk control for all trunk-family screens.
slots.validates: The current branch can reproduce the seq4096 trunk regime on this hardware.
slots.falsifies: Any trunk-family screen is untrustworthy because the control itself is drifting.

slots.name: trunk_fp16_embed_control
slots.why: Shared control for branches that naturally sit on top of fp16 tied-embedding export.
slots.validates: Whether fp16 embedding export alone is already a useful step over the clean trunk.
slots.falsifies: The fp16 export path is not strong enough to serve as a child-control.
notes: This control both tests fp16 export and serves as the matched parent for Muon WD and adaptive Muon.

slots.name: trunk_plus_sliding_eval64
slots.why: Tests whether eval-only context remains a first-order gain on the clean trunk.
slots.validates: Final BPB improves enough to justify keeping eval policy as an active frontier.
slots.falsifies: Sliding eval is too small or too expensive to matter in our budgeted search loop.
notes: Compared directly against the trunk control.

slots.name: trunk_plus_quant_quality
slots.why: Direct transfer test for our strongest quant-fidelity mutation from earlier runs.
slots.validates: The post-quant gap shrinks on real H100 screens.
slots.falsifies: The quant-fidelity win was mostly proxy noise or regime-specific.
notes: Compared directly against the trunk control.

slots.name: trunk_fp16_plus_muon_wd
slots.why: Tests whether the Muon WD story transfers once fp16 embed export is already present.
slots.validates: Muon WD improves final BPB or tightens the quantization gap over the fp16 control.
slots.falsifies: Muon WD adds complexity without moving the controlled fp16 branch.
notes: Matched against the fp16 export control, not the raw trunk.

slots.name: trunk_fp16_plus_adaptive_muon
slots.why: Tests whether lower step cost buys enough extra steps to help the fp16 trunk branch.
slots.validates: step_avg falls and final BPB holds or improves against the fp16 control.
slots.falsifies: The throughput gain is too small or the quality loss dominates again.
notes: This is the main throughput reclaim candidate.

slots.name: record_like_control_2048_int6
slots.validates: We can evaluate derivative mutations in the same qualitative regime as the current record stack.
slots.falsifies: The record-like branch itself is drifting too much to support derivative comparisons.

slots.name: record_like_plus_adaptive_muon
slots.why: Tests whether our adaptive-Muon win is a derivative improvement on top of a record-like public stack.
slots.validates: The hybrid beats or matches the record-like control while improving step efficiency.
slots.falsifies: Our throughput winner does not transfer onto the public-style branch.
notes: This is the strongest public-stack derivative screen in the pack.
